contentBlocks.original:
कराग्रे वसते लक्ष्मीः करमध्ये सरस्वति
करमूले तु गोविन्दः प्रभाते करदर्शनम्
contentBlocks.transliteration:
karāgre vasate lakṣmiḥ karamadhye sarasvati
karamūle tu govindaḥ prabhāte karadarśanam
contentBlocks.translation: On the tip of the hands resides Lakṣmī (the Goddess of Wealth), in the middle of the hands resides Sarasvatī (the Goddess of Knowledge), and at the base of the hands resides Govinda (Lord Vishnu). Therefore, one should look at one's hands in the morning.
contentBlocks.meanings: कराग्रे (karāgre) – on the tip of the hands (kara:hand | agre:on the tip); वसते (vasate) – resides; लक्ष्मीः (lakṣmīḥ) – Lakṣmī (Goddess of Wealth); करमध्ये (karamadhye) – in the middle of the hands (kara:hand | madhye:in the middle); सरस्वति (sarasvati) – Sarasvatī (Goddess of Knowledge); करमूले (karamūle) – at the base of the hands (kara:hand | mūle:at the base); तु (tu) – but; गोविन्दः (govindaḥ) – Govinda (Lord Vishnu); प्रभाते (prabhāte) – in the morning; करदर्शनम् (karadarśanam) – looking at one's hands (kara:hand | darśanam:looking at);
